notes: Scenario-package probability profiles preserve coupled drivers by referencing packaged archetype-library templates as coherent support points.
These profiles publish cumulative probability bounds over scenario packages, not over independent parameter marginals.
Tier C scenario-package outputs remain screening summaries and must not be interpreted as validated population exposure distributions.

profiles.label: Adult leave-on hand cream use-intensity package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled hand-cream use intensity packages for adult screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower coupled-use package with reduced amount and frequency.
Reference coupled-use package aligned to the baseline screening archetype.
Upper coupled-use package with higher amount and frequency.

limitations: This package varies multiple use-intensity drivers together but still represents only three packaged support points.
The profile is not a validated consumer frequency distribution.

profiles.label: Adult trigger spray room-context package
profiles.description: Probability bounds over coupled room and use-context packages for trigger-spray inhalation screening.

supportPoints.note: Larger-room and lower-release package for lower inhalation screening.
Reference room-context package aligned to the baseline trigger-spray archetype.
Smaller-room and higher-release package for upper inhalation screening.

limitations: This package preserves coupled room-volume, ventilation, duration, and release settings but does not resolve breathing-zone peaks.
The profile is not a validated indoor-microenvironment distribution.
